primary_accounting_issue: Whether intragroup dividend receivables creating foreign currency exposure in consolidated financial statements can qualify as hedged items under IFRS 9 hedge accounting

primary_authority.reason: Section 6.3 establishes what can be designated as hedged items; paragraph 6.3.6 specifically addresses intragroup monetary items and their eligibility for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements

supporting_authority.reason: Governs recognition of exchange differences on monetary items and clarifies when FX gains/losses on intragroup items are not eliminated on consolidation, which is the prerequisite for hedge eligibility under IFRS 9 6.3.6
Provides guidance on hedges of net investments in foreign operations, which is an alternative model when the dividend receivable relates to a net investment with FX exposure

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosures about subsidiaries and non-controlling interests but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items
Deals with related party disclosure requirements rather than hedge accounting treatment for FX exposures
Addresses non-reciprocal distributions of assets but does not address hedging of related FX exposures

treatment_families.family: Intragroup monetary items as eligible hedged items under general hedge accounting
Net investment hedge accounting for FX exposure on foreign operations
Recognition of FX differences without hedge accounting

approaches.label: General intragroup monetary hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6, intragroup monetary items (including dividend receivables) creating FX exposure not eliminated on consolidation may be designated as hedged items, provided all hedge accounting criteria in IFRS 9 are met

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: When the intragroup dividend receivable arises from a net investment in a foreign operation, IFRIC 16 permits hedge accounting for the foreign currency risk, with FX differences initially recognized in OCI and reclassified to P&L on disposal

approaches.label: FX recognition without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 paragraph 28 requires FX differences on monetary items to be recognized in P&L as they arise; hedge accounting is optional and not required if the entity does not designate a hedging relationship
